strengths: TypeScript/Node.js depth across Optilyz (3 yrs), RIDE, WatchUp, and re:cinq demonstrates consistent, multi-year backend competency
AI agent and MCP infrastructure work at re:cinq directly matches the agentic development requirement — a rare and differentiating qualification
AWS CDK at Optilyz demonstrates infrastructure-as-code capability within the required AWS ecosystem
concerns: NestJS, Vitest, and Playwright — all explicitly required by the JD — have zero evidence anywhere in the resume or project history
Kubernetes is entirely absent; Docker is present but K8s is an explicit requirement with no transferable evidence

A 9-month unexplained career gap (Oct 2025–present) combined with a pattern of short tenures (re:cinq 6 months, RIDE 15 months, WatchUp 12 months) raises unresolved questions about continuity and commitment
response.justification: The candidate's resume contains zero evidence of NestJS, Vitest, or Playwright — all explicitly listed JD requirements — and Kubernetes is similarly absent despite being a named infrastructure requirement. Java knowledge is approximately 8 years stale and Kotlin is nowhere present, eliminating coverage of the secondary language requirement. PostgreSQL, auth/security implementations, and GCP/Azure experience are also unsubstantiated. The most recent role lasted only 6 months and is followed by a 9-month unexplained gap, creating compounded uncertainty about current technical currency and reliability. While the AI agent and TypeScript/Node.js experience are genuine differentiators, the volume and severity of hard-requirement gaps — particularly the core framework and testing toolchain — make this candidate a weak fit for the role as specified.
